source_pr.title: ddl, test: fix deadloop in test about doDDLJob
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #19798 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed: revert some changes made in #18844, which may cause deadloop 

How it works: #18844 faces a problem of data race at `d.ctx`, in fact add `// +build !race` is enough, other changes wrongly changed the behaviour of ddl workers and `doDDLJobs`, which may cause a deadloop and weaken the code.

NOTE that `doDDLJob` will block if ddl worker accidentally exit (but stop block if restart later), at waiting `task.err` channel. If we want to resolve this block, such as exit when context canceled, it will cause restarted ddl worker of the sending end's block. Sending end's block can't be resolved by detecting context canceled in same way, because `d.ctx` may be replaced thus new context is not canceled at that time.
So I just hope ddl worker won't accidentally exit

### Related changes

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test

Side effects

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

<!-- Please write a release note here to describe the change you made when it is released to the users of TiDB. If your PR doesn't involve any change to TiDB(like test enhancements, RFC proposals...), you can write `No release note`. -->
- No release note

symptoms: DDL restart tests can hang/deadloop while waiting for `doDDLJob` / history job completion during worker restarts.
failure_signature: Potential endless loop / hang in `ddl/restart_test.go` when polling history DDL job after `doDDLJob` returns `context.Canceled`
root_cause_explanation: In `ddl/restart_test.go`, `runInterruptedJob` could enter an unbounded sleep/poll loop when `doDDLJob` returns `context.Canceled` during DDL worker restarts and the job is not found in history yet. That can lead to a deadloop/hang (or CI timeout). This PR stabilizes the test by handling `context.Canceled` explicitly: polling history with a sleep interval but adding a hard 1-minute deadline to avoid endless loops, and aligning restart/worker behavior so tasks can complete and report errors deterministically.
fix_pattern: Make async waits bounded: handle `context.Canceled` in interrupted DDL job tests and add a maximum wait deadline to prevent endless polling loops.

evidence: evidence: PR title: ddl, test: fix deadloop in test about doDDLJob
evidence: handle `context.Canceled` and add 1-minute guard to avoid endless loop while waiting history job (ddl/restart_test.go)
